problem.title: Valid Palindrome
problem.description: A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers
solutions.description: Two pointers start at opposite ends of the string and skip non-alphanumeric characters before comparing the characters they point to; a mismatch immediately returns false. Advancing both pointers inward after each successful comparison covers the entire string in one pass. The key insight is that skipping invalid characters in-place avoids building a cleaned copy of the string, keeping space at O(1).
